title: Signed Language Writing Critical Review Series Overview
subtitle: Scope, method, structure, reading paths, and future direction for the review series
summary: Full-path review of signed-language writing, notation, encoding, standards, tools, datasets, and infrastructure claims.
synopsis: The review series asks what problem each source actually solves and separates layer-level success from full-path adequacy for signed-language literacy as durable written text. Its method, structure, review set, reading paths, recurring critique categories, and future direction keep useful contributions separate from unresolved writing, encoding, rendering, data-governance, authority, and infrastructure gaps.

slides.title: What This Series Reviews
slides.summary: Writing, notation, encoding, standards, tools, datasets, and infrastructure claims can be reviewed through a problem-specific full-path method.

image_prompts.concrete: Critical review overview slide showing literature, notation systems, encoding proposals, standards records, datasets, and SignWriting evaluated through layer-level and full-path judgments
image_prompts.abstract: A set of reviewed sources passing through a structured evaluation frame

slides.speech: This is the review series. It evaluates literature, theses, proposals, standards records, tools, datasets, and recurring claims about signed-language writing through a shared full-path method.

slides.title: Method
slides.summary: The review method separates writing, notation, encoding, rendering, infrastructure, evidence, authority, and data objects.

image_prompts.concrete: Method slide with layers labeled writing, notation, encoding, rendering, infrastructure, evidence, authority, and data objects
image_prompts.abstract: A review lens separating overlapping layers

bullets: Separate layer-level success from full-path adequacy
Name what problem each source actually solves
Credit strengths as well as limits
slides.speech: The method is to separate layer-level success from full-path adequacy before judging claims. A source may be strong on notation without solving signed-language literacy as written text. It may solve rendering without solving encoding. The review series credits strengths, names limits, and makes critique constructive.
slides.notes: This slide should make the series feel fair.

slides.title: Reading Paths
slides.summary: Readers can enter through the method, internal baseline, core external reviews, broader theory, or construction priorities.

image_prompts.concrete: Review reading map with paths for method, baseline, core literature, broader theory, and construction priorities
image_prompts.abstract: Multiple scholarly paths through a review field

slides.speech: The series has several valid paths. New readers can start with the overview. Method readers can go to the full-path critique framework. Literature readers can enter through the external reviews. Readers looking for next steps can go to the construction-priorities entry.
slides.notes: Make the large review series feel navigable.

slides.title: Review Commitments
slides.summary: The series credits real strengths, names limits, separates layer-level success from full-path adequacy, and avoids all-or-nothing judgments.

image_prompts.concrete: Scholarly commitments slide showing balanced scales for crediting strengths, naming limits, separating layer-level success from full-path adequacy, and avoiding all-or-nothing judgments
image_prompts.abstract: Review principles arranged as a fair evaluation compass

bullets: Separate layer-level success from full-path adequacy
Use review to support better construction
slides.speech: The review commitments are simple. Credit real strengths before critique. Name limits without turning the source into a caricature. Separate layer-level success from full-path adequacy. Use review to support better writing systems, tools, standards, data practice, and research.
slides.notes: This completes the overview by naming the series posture. It should make the reviews feel fair, disciplined, and useful.
